Scenario.title: SolarWinds-Inspired SCADA System Compromise in Energy Sector
Scenario.overview: Energy Standard A sophisticated attack chain targeting energy sector SCADA systems through vulnerability exploitation and firmware tampering, mirroring SolarWinds' supply chain compromise methodology to disrupt grid operations.

threat_actor_profile.motivation: Operational disruption and potential financial extortion

Scenario.attack_surface: AVAILABLE_SURFACES: ["Improperly configured access control lists", "SSL Certificates and Attribution", "Shared directory for operational logs", "Internet-facing SCADA systems", "Public cloud-stored operational data", "Custom APIs for IoT grid sensors"]

stage.name: Initial Access
stage.description: Attackers targeted exposed industrial control systems commonly used in energy grids, exploiting a known vulnerability (CVE-2016-4171) in SCADA interfaces to gain initial foothold, mimicking SolarWinds' supply chain approach by compromising trusted infrastructure components.

stage.name: Execution
stage.surface: Custom APIs for IoT grid sensors
stage.capec: CAPEC-657: Malicious Automated Software Update via Spoofing
stage.description: Threat actors injected backdoors into firmware updates for smart grid devices through compromised API endpoints, analogous to SolarWinds' code insertion into software builds, allowing persistent control over IoT grid sensors.

stage.name: Persistence
stage.description: Stolen credentials were stored in shared directories accessible to maintenance teams, enabling long-term access and lateral movement within the network, similar to SolarWinds' persistence mechanisms through credential harvesting.

stage.name: Privilege Escalation
stage.capec: CAPEC-678: System Build Data Maliciously Altered
stage.description: Attackers exploited overly permissive admin roles in energy management systems through password spraying attacks, escalating privileges to gain full control over critical infrastructure, mirroring SolarWinds' credential escalation tactics.

stage.name: Defense Evasion
stage.description: Command-and-control traffic was encrypted using valid SSL certificates to mimic legitimate traffic patterns, evading detection mechanisms akin to SolarWinds' stealthy communication strategies through TLS manipulation.

stage.name: Impact
stage.description: Critical grid operation data stored in public clouds was encrypted using ransomware, causing operational disruptions and demanding payment, directly reflecting SolarWinds' focus on maximizing operational impact through data encryption.

Scenario.summary: This scenario outlines a multi-stage attack targeting energy sector SCADA systems through initial vulnerability exploitation, firmware tampering, credential theft, privilege escalation, and ransomware deployment, following a methodology reminiscent of the SolarWinds compromise to achieve maximum operational disruption.
